title: Add a `new-task` helper script
description: Create a small Node or Python script (whichever fits the rest of the project) that scaffolds a new task JSON: picks the next sequential key, prompts for title/description/acceptance_criteria/priority, stamps `created_at`/`updated_at`, and writes the file plus updates `index.json`. This keeps the human-driven path low-friction so we actually use the local store.
acceptance_criteria: Running the script with no arguments prompts for required fields and produces a valid task JSON that conforms to `tasks/schema.json`.
The new task's key is exactly one greater than the highest existing TASK-NNN file.
`tasks/index.json` is updated atomically (write to temp file, rename) so a crash mid-write cannot corrupt the index.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Picked up after TASK-001 closed. Design: programmatic `createTask({...})` exported from `src/task-store.js` (reuses the existing `readAllTasks` + `atomicWriteFiles` + `buildIndexBytes` helpers — index write is already atomic via the two-phase primitive landed in TASK-001) plus a thin CLI wrapper at `bin/new-task.js` with `node:readline` for TTY prompts and flag-based non-interactive mode. Prompter is injectable so tests run with no I/O. Workflow: developer writes failing Vitest suite covering next-key derivation, schema conformance, atomic index write, and the CLI prompter loop, then implements both layers, then reviewer in fresh context.
